spec_match.rationale: Uses `::column` on a real multi-column container (`columns: 3 14ch`), applies only post-layout properties (box-shadow, background), and re-renders when count / gap sliders change.

interactive.rationale: Two range sliders (column count, column gap) and a mode-toggle button drive a live multi-column reel — every input change re-renders the columns and the ::column outline.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises drag count/gap sliders, watch ::column outline each column box, toggle outlined/tinted — delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Slider goes down to count=1 — at that point there's only one column and `::column` outlines a single box. Worth surfacing that. Doesn't show column-fill effects (balance vs auto).

references.rationale: Links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5192758898589696 — that ID does not match this feature (correct: 5192332683771904). The slug-recovery script reads this ID; a wrong ID risks the folder being mis-slugged or deleted.

design_system.rationale: All colours via CSS variables and color-mix; warn-block for unsupported state; no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Fix the wrong chromestatus reference ID
openQuestions.detail: Page links chromestatus 5192758898589696. The correct ID for ::column pseudo-element is 5192332683771904. The cleanup script reads this ID to recover folder names; a wrong ID risks deletion or mis-slug. Update the see-also link.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate column-fill effects
openQuestions.detail: `column-fill: balance` vs `column-fill: auto` changes how content distributes across columns. Add a toggle so authors can see whether all ::column outlines have content or only the leading ones fill.

openQuestions.title: Try a non-applicable property to teach the constraint
openQuestions.detail: Add a `try width: 50px on ::column` button that injects a layout-affecting property. The spec says it's a no-op. Showing this teaches the most important constraint of the pseudo.

openQuestions.title: Animate transitions between mode toggles
openQuestions.detail: Currently mode toggle snaps from outline to tinted. Use `transition: box-shadow 0.2s, background 0.2s` on `::column` (verify spec allows transitions on pseudo-elements — that's a separate spec ticket) for a polished reveal.

summary: Solid live-editor with real sliders and feature detect, but the chromestatus reference ID points at a different feature. Urgent fix; everything else is polish.
